Description: PicklePlay
Technical Detail: Name appears in the top bar, login hero, and PWA manifest.
Notes: Branded as a pickleball discovery and social play app.

Description: Source code location for the active app.
Technical Detail: /var/public/pickleplay/app
Notes: Documentation is based on this checkout.

Description: Mobile-first progressive web app for pickleball players.
Technical Detail: React single page app built with Vite and installed-capable PWA configuration.
Notes: UI is optimized around phone-sized screens but uses responsive desktop constraints.

Description: Help users discover courts, find games, create games, invite players, and join clubs.
Technical Detail: Main flows are implemented as separate screen components under src/screens.
Notes: Current implementation behaves like a product prototype with mock data.

Description: Recreational pickleball players, club members, game organizers, and players looking for venues.
Technical Detail: Domain entities include Court, Game, Club, User, and Message.
Notes: The app centers on local play coordination rather than tournament administration.

Description: Modern React and TypeScript application.
Technical Detail: React 19, TypeScript, Vite, Tailwind CSS v4, lucide-react, Leaflet, React Leaflet, Zustand dependency.
Notes: Zustand and React Router are installed but the current app mainly uses local React state routing.

Description: Supports installable standalone app behavior.
Technical Detail: vite-plugin-pwa manifest includes app icons, portrait orientation, standalone display, and auto-update service worker registration.
Notes: Runtime cache is configured for OpenStreetMap tile assets.

Description: App state determines the active screen instead of browser URL routes.
Technical Detail: src/App.tsx defines the Screen union, navigate, goBack, tab handling, login flow, and onboarding flow.
Notes: Browser deep-linking is not currently represented in the route model.

Description: Persistent bottom navigation for the core authenticated app.
Technical Detail: Tabs: Home, Nearby, Games, Clubs, Profile.
Notes: Login, onboarding, search, notifications, and filter screens hide the main chrome.

Description: App-wide shortcuts for search, notifications, and game creation.
Technical Detail: Top bar exposes search and notifications; FAB opens create-game on Home and Games.
Notes: Top bar is hidden for modal/fullscreen utility screens.

Description: Demo login state controls access to the app.
Technical Detail: isLoggedIn state in src/App.tsx; login success routes to onboarding or home.
Notes: No backend auth call is wired in the current screen implementation.

Description: First-session setup collects player readiness information.
Technical Detail: Local onboarding state includes location and skill-level step UI.
Notes: Completion toggles needsOnboarding false in memory only.

Description: Screens use locally defined demo arrays and local component state.
Technical Detail: Shared interfaces live in src/lib/types.ts; screen cards currently do not fetch from API endpoints.
Notes: Backend integration remains a future implementation layer.

Description: Development proxy exists for future API calls.
Technical Detail: Vite proxies /api to http://localhost:3001.
Notes: No active screen data loader was found using this proxy.

Description: Nearby discovery uses map/list presentation.
Technical Detail: Leaflet and React Leaflet render Austin-centered map data and support locate behavior.
Notes: User geolocation is invoked through Leaflet map.locate().

Description: Playful sports/social UI with bold color accents.
Technical Detail: Tailwind theme defines electric blue primary, neon lime secondary, coral tertiary, surface hierarchy, typography, spacing, and shadows in src/index.css.
Notes: Design language uses rounded chips/buttons, cards, bottom tabs, and mobile touch targets.

Description: The app models venues, players, games, clubs, and messages.
Technical Detail: Type definitions cover court metadata, user profile, game scheduling, club membership, and message records.
Notes: Types are ready for API-backed entities but sample UI still uses static objects.

Description: Standard Vite development and production scripts.
Technical Detail: npm run dev, npm run build, npm run lint, npm run preview.
Notes: npm run build runs TypeScript build before Vite bundling.

Description: Local development server configuration.
Technical Detail: Port 9000, host 0.0.0.0, allowed host pickleplay-pwa.eunika.xyz.
Notes: Useful for previewing from the configured external hostname.

Description: Feature-complete prototype shell with strong UI coverage.
Technical Detail: Major user journeys are present, but persistence, real search, real filters, auth, and backend mutation are not wired.
Notes: Treat as frontend prototype documentation unless backend work is later connected.
